rule.title: Quality Gate Standards
rule.description:
Understand and enforce quality gate conditions:
TYPICAL QUALITY GATE CONDITIONS
1. Reliability - New Bugs = 0 (on new code) - Reliability Rating = A (on new code)
2. Security - New Vulnerabilities = 0 (on new code) - Security Rating = A (on new code) - Security Hotspots Reviewed = 100%
3. Maintainability - New Code Smells < threshold (e.g., 5) - Maintainability Rating = A (on new code) - Technical Debt Ratio < 5%
4. Coverage - Coverage on New Code >= 80% - Line Coverage >= 80% - Branch Coverage >= 70%
5. Duplications - Duplicated Lines on New Code < 3% - Duplicated Blocks < threshold
QUALITY GATE STATUS - PASSED: All conditions met - FAILED: One or more conditions not met - ERROR: Analysis failed or incomplete

rule.title: Quality Gate Failure Analysis
rule.description:
When quality gate fails, analyze and report:
1. IDENTIFY FAILING CONDITIONS - List each condition that failed - Show actual vs. expected values - Indicate severity of failure
2. CATEGORIZE FAILURES - Critical: Security vulnerabilities, blocker bugs - High: Major bugs, low coverage - Medium: Code smells, minor duplications
3. PROVIDE CONTEXT - Show trend: improving or degrading - Compare with previous analysis - Identify affected modules/files
4. SUGGEST ACTIONS - Prioritize fixes by impact - Provide specific remediation steps - Estimate effort required
5. REPORT FORMAT ``` ❌ Quality Gate: FAILED
Failing Conditions: 1. [CRITICAL] New Vulnerabilities: 2 (Expected: 0) - Fix security issues in AuthService.java - Update vulnerable dependencies
2. [HIGH] Coverage on New Code: 65% (Expected: ≥80%) - Add tests for PaymentService - Increase test coverage in OrderController
3. [MEDIUM] Code Smells: 8 (Expected: <5) - Reduce complexity in processOrder() - Extract duplicate code in validation methods ```

rule.title: Quality Gate Success Reporting
rule.description:
When quality gate passes, provide comprehensive report:
✅ Quality Gate: PASSED
All conditions met: - ✅ New Bugs: 0 - ✅ New Vulnerabilities: 0 - ✅ Security Hotspots Reviewed: 100% - ✅ Coverage on New Code: 85% - ✅ Duplicated Lines: 1.2%
Quality Ratings: - Reliability: A - Security: A - Maintainability: A
Key Metrics: - Total Issues: 12 (down from 18) - Technical Debt: 2d 4h (reduced by 1d) - Code Coverage: 82%
Recommendations: - Continue monitoring security hotspots - Maintain test coverage above 80% - Address remaining minor code smells

rule.title: Custom Quality Gate Configuration
rule.description:
Suggest custom quality gates based on project needs:
STRICT GATE (High-Security Projects) - Zero tolerance for vulnerabilities - 90% coverage requirement - No blocker/critical issues - Security hotspots 100% reviewed
STANDARD GATE (Enterprise Projects) - No new vulnerabilities - 80% coverage on new code - Reliability rating A - Technical debt ratio < 5%
LENIENT GATE (Legacy Modernization) - Focus on new code quality - 70% coverage on new code - No new blocker bugs - Gradual improvement targets
STARTUP GATE (Fast-Moving Projects) - Balance speed and quality - 75% coverage - No critical security issues - Manageable technical debt
